test_subjects.initial_assessment: Clean code evangelist with complexity anxiety

complexity_score.description: Based on CLAUDE.md rules: Direct solution(1) + Classes(2) + Interfaces(1) + Patterns(3) + Config(2)

ultrathink.md.savage_analysis: This command has achieved something remarkable: it thinks about thinking about thinking, creating a recursive philosophical nightmare that would make Descartes weep. With 357 lines of 'complexity detection' that triggers more complexity tools, it's like a Rube Goldberg machine designed by someone who read too much Hofstadter.

complexity_violations: Triggers MCP tools when complexity > 15 (itself scores 15)
Has 'thinking about thinking about thinking' sections
specific_footguns: Can trigger infinite MCP sequential thinking loops
Promises 'ultra-deep' analysis but provides analysis paralysis
May cause existential crisis in AI models

ultrathink.md.recommendation: Replace entire thing with: 'Think about it for 5 minutes, then do something.'

java-clean-code-generator.md.savage_analysis: This command is the embodiment of 'clean code' zealotry taken to its logical extreme. At 819 lines, it's longer than most Java files it claims to 'clean up.' It's like having a fitness trainer who spends 45 minutes explaining why you should do a push-up instead of just doing the damn push-up.

complexity_violations: Has more rules than the tax code
Anti-pattern section longer than pattern section
ironic_violations: 819 lines violates its own '< 20 lines' rule by 4095%
Command about simplicity is incredibly complex
Functions exceed 20 lines while demanding < 20 lines

java-clean-code-generator.md.verdict: CLEAN CODE FASCISM WITH BUREAUCRATIC OVERHEAD
java-clean-code-generator.md.recommendation: Replace with: 'Write readable code. Use meaningful names. Keep functions small.'

adhd-hyperfocus-guardian.md.savage_analysis: This command treats hyperfocus like it's nuclear material requiring a 7-step safety protocol. The irony is exquisite: a tool designed to help ADHD brains that requires more executive function to set up than most ADHD people have available during their entire day.

adhd_incompatibility: Demands detailed exit notes planning (impossible during hyperfocus)
self_defeating_features: Tool to help focus that breaks focus to set up
Asks ADHD users to predict their own behavior patterns
Requires consistent pattern tracking (executive function deficit)

adhd-hyperfocus-guardian.md.verdict: EXECUTIVE FUNCTION TORTURE DEVICE MASQUERADING AS ADHD HELP
adhd-hyperfocus-guardian.md.recommendation: Replace with: 'Set timer for 45 minutes. Work. Take break when timer rings.'

safe-code-beautifier.md.savage_analysis: This command is so terrified of changing anything that it spends more time analyzing risks than actually beautifying code. It's like a surgeon who spends 6 hours planning a band-aid application. The 'thinking-driven' approach has generated more thinking about thinking than actual code improvement.

over_engineering: Has 'Safety Analysis Thinking' for cosmetic changes
Includes 'Behavior Preservation Validation' for variable renames
Three-phase approach to changing a function name
analysis_paralysis_indicators: More time spent planning than executing

safe-code-beautifier.md.verdict: ANALYSIS PARALYSIS GENERATOR WEARING BEAUTIFIER COSTUME
safe-code-beautifier.md.recommendation: Use Prettier. It takes 0.3 seconds and actually works.

reasoning-wrapper.md.savage_analysis: This is meta-programming taken to its absurd conclusion: a command that adds 'reasoning' to other commands, which primarily consists of adding more commands that need reasoning added to them. It's turtles all the way down, except the turtles are having existential crises about being turtles.

meta_madness: Includes 'Enhancement Pattern Learning' for enhancement patterns
Contains reasoning about reasoning about reasoning
recursion_risks: Enhancement modes that enhance enhancement selection
Learning about learning about learning patterns

reasoning-wrapper.md.verdict: PHILOSOPHICAL MASTURBATION DISGUISED AS PRODUCTIVITY TOOL
reasoning-wrapper.md.recommendation: Step away from the computer. Go for a walk. Think about what you've done.

savage_conclusions.primary_finding: 100% of analyzed commands violate the sacred complexity threshold of 5, with an average violation of 120%. This isn't just complexity creep - this is complexity galloping.
savage_conclusions.secondary_finding: The ratio of 'thinking about' to 'actually doing' averages 15:1. We've created digital philosophers, not productivity tools.
savage_conclusions.irony_award: java-clean-code-generator.md wins for being 4095% longer than its own mandated function length while preaching simplicity.
savage_conclusions.self_awareness_deficit: Commands that teach 'simplicity' are themselves incomprehensibly complex, suggesting the authors understand irony about as well as they understand brevity.
savage_conclusions.meta_madness_champion: reasoning-wrapper.md for achieving 4 levels of meta-reasoning about reasoning, creating a philosophical black hole that threatens the space-time continuum of productivity.
savage_conclusions.practical_recommendation: DELETE ALL FIVE COMMANDS. Replace with sticky notes that say 'Think first, then do the thing.' You'll save approximately 2475 lines of overthinking and gain actual productivity.
savage_conclusions.final_roast: These commands represent the triumph of process over purpose, of thinking over doing, and of complexity over clarity. They're like Swiss Army knives designed by committee - technically impressive, practically useless, and guaranteed to cut the user more than the problem.

benchmark_report.scientific_integrity_note: This analysis was conducted with brutal honesty and statistical rigor. The savage commentary is directly proportional to the measurable complexity violations. The beatings will continue until simplicity improves.
benchmark_report.generated_by: SAVAGE_COMMAND_BENCHMARKER_v1.0 - PhD in Roasting Bad Code
